p_text:
I love a big, hardcover cookbook marked with Post-It Notes and dog-eared pages. There’s something so satisfying about pulling a cookbook off a shelf and reading through a great recipe that’s tangible in your hands and not on your iPad. I prefer it! A few weeks ago, I was gifted an early copy of Cookie Club, a gorgeous cookbook devoted entirely to cookies. Turning the pages, I immediately fell in love with the cozy writing, helpful cookie-baking tips (cookie spread too much? Not enough flour!), creative hosting ideas, and little paintings sprinkled throughout. Of course, I wanted to bake every cookie (there are 80!) immediately. Cookie Club author Mallory Oiniki devotes her entire first chapter to the art of the perfect chocolate chip cookie — thick and gooey, thin and crispy, and the Goldilocks: chewy with pools of melted chocolate (my personal favorite - that recipe, ahead!). The second chapter covers the classics, like M&M, oatmeal raisin, and double chocolate. Then Cookie Club diverts delightfully left-of-center, and you get cookies like miso toffee snickerdoodle, crème brûlée (yep, flagged that immediately!), and banana pudding, which is basically Magnolia Banana Pudding in cookie form. Mallory fires on all cylinders. There’s a chapter in the book titled ‘Cookies Inspired by TV Shows and Movies’ that feels very BS-aligned. Every time I flip through Cookie Club, I find another thing to love. Last week, I jumped on a Zoom with Mallory to dig into how she built a career around cookies (my dream?), her creative inspiration, plus, she drops her go-to baking playlist and the best chocolate chip cookie recipe from her new cookbook. Beau Appétit! I’m so inspired by people like you who take something they love, like baking cookies, and make a full career out of it. How did you find your way to writing a cookie cookbook? I’ve always loved cookies. Everyone loves cookies! They’re such a shareable treat. Some baked goods are just too scary for people for good reason, if they’re really complicated or technical. But cookies...everyone can at least try to make a cookie. They’re so approachable, and that’s something I really gravitated towards. In 2020, when we were all at home making banana bread and sourdough, I started posting my recipes online, but never with the intention of turning it into a career. I’ve always loved cooking and baking, so it started as just a fun hobby. Then I started the series that I called ‘Cookies Inspired by TV Shows and Movies.’ I did eight or nine cookies in that series, and they all popped off and went super viral. That was so fun! I would take any element of the show or movie I was watching and try to find a cookie idea that fit naturally with it, but that was creative and out of the box. I did that series for about six months, briefly stopped it, and brought it back in the book, which I’m so excited about. There are a bunch of new ones! So I say I kind of stumbled into my dream job. I definitely think it was a lot of luck and good timing, as I think a lot of things in life are. I could not be more grateful. I always wanted to write a cookbook, but I just thought that wouldn’t happen for a long time, so when the opportunity presented itself, I was over the moon. And obviously, cookies were what I was most excited about. I post savory foods as well and share those recipes, but cookies are definitely what I’m best known for, so that made the most sense to turn into a cookbook, and I’m so glad I did it. Your ‘Cookies Inspired by TV Shows and Movies’ kind of put you on the map! What’s the cookie inspired by a TV or movie that went most viral? The first one I made was a Stranger Things cookie because that show was big at the time. Then I just started asking people in the comments about what TV show or movie I should do next. Gilmore Girls was always at the top of the list. I had never seen Gilmore Girls. I grew up with three older brothers, so I didn’t have a lot of control over the TV. But once I started working on this series, I dove in and binged it, and was like, obviously this cookie has to be something around coffee, and so a Gilmore Girls Coffee Cake Cookie was perfect. That’s probably still the most popular cookie on my website. I’ve gotten so many DMs from people being like, “I made these and watched the show with my mom, and it was such a sweet experience.” It’s so fun to see how people use the cookies to connect with someone, and then the show or movie also connects them. What’s the cookie inspired by a movie or TV show that you could eat every day? Taste-wise, one of my favorites is the Ted Lasso Shortbread Cookies. They’re everything you want in shortbread: buttery, flaky, with turbinado sugar on top so you get that crunch. I could eat so many of those. And they’re not too sweet. It’s kind of ironic that I wrote a cookie cookbook because I don’t like overly sweet things. My cookies have a decent amount of salt because I like a balanced cookie, not an overly sweet cookie that you can only take one bite of. But shortbread especially is that way because it’s so butter-focused. Those are really fun ones. They’re so cute when you put them in a little box like in Ted Lasso. But I mean also, and truly, this is gonna sound like I’m sucking up, but Sinners is incredible, and it would be so fun to make a cookie inspired by Sinners. I’d love to try that cookie! You also write about the art of gathering around cookies, which I love. Talk to me about how to throw a cookie party. We so often associate cookie parties with the holidays, which is great, but there are so many cookie flavors that aren’t holiday-specific that are perfect for hosting. One of the parties I love that I talk about in the book is a ‘Girls Night In.’ In that chapter, there’s a picture of me with a tub of my edible cookie dough, which is a really popular recipe and everything I want in a girls’ night in. In that chapter, I also include my Banana Pudding Cookies, which are really fun and cozy. If you like Magnolia Banana Pudding, it’s essentially that in cookie form. It’s a vanilla wafer cookie that’s really soft, rolled in vanilla wafer crumbs, topped with a pudding frosting, and fresh bananas on top. I think, and I write about this in the book, that cookies are the perfect treat to connect with people and find that common ground we need a lot more of nowadays. You also have a section in the book about ‘Cookies For Your Star Sign’ — so fun! What was that process like, creating cookies based on astrology? It was so fun to write that section! I know a decent amount about star signs, but I really had to dive deep to write that section and figure out the intricacies. That would be a really fun cookie party as well! Have people come over and bring the cookie of their star sign. People are so proud of their sign and have strong ownership over it. What’s your star sign cookie? I’m a Virgo, and it’s an Almond Croissant Cookie — I’m not just saying this, it’s really good. Virgos love perfection and orderliness, and the Almond Croissant Cookie essentially tastes like an almond croissant; it has frangipane in the dough and slivered almonds, and it’s dusted with powdered sugar. It’s a very pristine and elegant cookie. If you love almond croissants, it’s for you. What sign are you? I’m a Sagittarius! Sagittarius is a Blood Orange Cardamom and Pistachio Cookie. They’re adventurous, always chasing the next thrill. They’re daring, bright, and always down to try something new! I love the illustrations in this book. Are they paintings? Most people use illustrations in cookbooks, but I wanted paintings. I have a friend from high school who’s an incredible artist, her name is Giovana Cavalcante, and her artwork handle is ‘I make art not sense.’ I’m obsessed with her stuff, so I just went to her and was like, hey, would you like to do some paintings for the book? She said yes, and she hand-painted all of these paintings, and we scanned them in. She did a really amazing job. She really captured the vibe I was going for. The painting in the Chocolate Chip Cookie chapter is of my daughter, who just turned four, reaching for a chocolate chip cookie. With a cookie cookbook, I hope it’s something people are baking from and using, but I also wanted it to be a beautiful book people want to leave out. I think the paintings really help with that. I love that! Does your daughter have a favorite cookie? She would eat anything. She loves the edible cookie dough and always wants to lick the bowl! The cookbook process takes a long time. I’ve been working on this for two years — 80 cookies is a lot! I had to test each cookie five-plus times to really get it right. So she tested a lot of the cookies with me. At four, she knows how to crack an egg and turn on and off the stand mixer! Most underrated kitchen tool: A baking scale makes a huge difference! You can get one for $30. I grew up using measuring cups, which work great too, but a kitchen scale makes your recipes a lot more accurate. If you want to level up your baking, that’s a really easy way to do it. And overrated: I’m not a big apron person. I just risk it. Unless I’m searing meat and I don’t want oil splattered on me, I just go for it. I can dust off the flour. Best baking playlist: Olivia Dean. It’s been Olivia Dean for years. I’m so excited for her growth and explosion. I love romanticizing cooking and baking, and turning on some music you love is a really great way to do that. A recipe you saved or screenshotted recently: This brothy bean recipe with miso and green onions. I’m dying to try it. You slow-cook beans for a couple of hours with miso paste. It’s savory and umami, and finished off with some scallions. I’ll be making that one soon. The secret to amazing food pics: I take all my pictures for my website and my normal content, but for a cookbook, you hire a full team. We had a photographer, a food stylist, and a food assistant. It was a whole operation. We made and photographed 80 cookies in 10 business days. It’s a lot. We used all the little tricks of food styling, like using makeup sponges to hold up cookies and make the perfect stack. [This makes me feel better about my own food photos!] A cookbook everyone should own: I love Molly Baz’s Cook This Book. Your “trust me, just try it!” cookie: The Chili Crisp Cookie is probably my most insane cookie. It’s really good — you really have to try it. I put the chili crisp in a brittle, so it’s contained because I didn’t want the whole cookie to be spicy and overwhelming. But you have these tiny bites of spice, then it’s paired with sesame seeds and coconut. That’s a really fun and pushes what people expect from a cookie. Baking advice that applies to life: Don’t take it too seriously. We’re all floating on a spinning rock. If you want to go for something in life, go for it. If you’re embarrassed, don’t be. No one really cares at the end of the day. Same with baking. People don’t try things because they think, oh, I can’t do that. But it just takes trying and putting yourself out there in any part of life, baking included. I feel so inspired by Mallory. If you love something, it can never be the wrong path. Go for it and don’t hold back. It reminds me of this letter. The one thing I was looking for was connection — actual, genuine connection, not the kind you get on social media, but a way to find a common ground with somebody. I hope you get a hard copy of Cookie Club (out April 14th!) and make cookies with or for someone you love! I plan to! I didn’t chill the dough for 12 hours (couldn’t wait!), but I will definitely be doing that next time. Make these ASAP and let me know how it goes. Also, I’m curious, what’s your baking playlist?? raw_article:
Hello, lovely people! How’s Daylight Saving treating you? I’m loving that it’s staying lighter later. It’s given me an unexpected burst of energy. On Monday, I asked our MBS (More BS) readers to ask me anything. The more we do these, the more I enjoy them.

For today, here are my thoughts and responses to a range of questions, from what I find most beautiful about spring to my go-to sushi order. (Looking forward to a sushi moment; it’s been a minute!) Plus, I offer a personal dessert suggestion for your Oscars watch party and talk about my audition process for The Edge of Seventeen. On a completely separate question, Woody Harrelson makes an appearance…

And for a little something extra, a peek inside one of my favorite days — my baby shower — coming early next week in More BS.

Subscribe now

Hope you enjoy!

1. Since Spring is approaching, what’s your favorite beauty that appears in March?? Personally, mine are the flowers and longer sunsets! They make me feel refreshed. (Plus my birthday, but that’s cheating!!) -Tailor

Like you, Tailor, I’m obsessed with the flowers, the longer days, and sunsets. There’s something beautiful about this time of year when there’s a light breeze in the air before the heat of summer. I really love this quote I read the other day: “Stop worrying. If February didn’t March, April May.” There’s this positive energy that comes with spring. It’s a natural reset. You can do your spring cleaning, if that’s what you’re into (I am). Plus, it’s your birthday! You’re allowed to love that!

2. What’s your sushi order? I’ve tried several (recommended by sushi lovers) and just can’t. I really want to like it. Help. Please! -Kathy

I’m not incredibly ambitious when it comes to sushi, but I do love it. It took someone who knew me very well to introduce me to sushi in a way that doesn’t turn me off. It’s easy for people who love sushi to recommend the extreme. They might say, “Just try the uni!” If that happened to me, I’d never go back. But I’m lucky that Josh helped ease me in. He was thoughtful about it. He’s a big sushi lover, but when we ate it together, he started with the approachable options, not with what he had worked his way up to. All to say, my favorite type of sushi is albacore nigiri, just fish over rice. Rolls tend to intimidate me if there’s too much going on. I do love a shrimp tempura roll, that’s one I’ll never turn down.

Also, I love that you're saying you really want to like it! I felt the same way and fully convinced myself that my social life would be better if I ate sushi. I feel like everyone's always going for sushi, and I wanted to partake. Now that I've eased in, I'd encourage you to get into it! Being in a great sushi restaurant is so cool. Sushi chefs are artists. There's an appreciation for the ritual. If there's ever an opportunity to try an omakase experience, do it! This is big talk coming from a non-ambitious sushi eater, but now I want to get everyone in my life into sushi. My brother is a hard sell, and now I'm like, how can I crack the code to get him to try it? There’s this restaurant I love that has wagyu nigiri, so it's not technically sushi. I think if I can get him to eat that, then he'll be in!

3. Would you ever consider playing a villain or an antagonist in a movie? -Hannah

Yes, of course! Know anyone hiring?

4. You’ve spoken so warmly about the cast of Sinners, what’s a moment from filming or press promo with them that still makes you smile?? -Soff

I want to use a specific moment to answer this question. But at this moment, being two days away from the Oscars, I know there is no one single particular moment that can encapsulate what this filming experience meant to me. These people have left a mark on me and on my heart that will never go away.

5. What were your auditions like for the movie The Edge of Seventeen? -Ryan

I don’t remember off the top of my head how many auditions I had. Let’s call it a handful, if that; it wasn’t anything crazy. The craziest part was that I was offered the job in the audition room. The last audition was just me, Kelly Fremon Craig, and James L. Brooks. I went through the reading, and I had this feeling that happens in a great audition, where I think, if nothing else happens from here, I’ll be fine. I’d be devastated for a minute, but I felt really good about what I did. Then Jim and Kelly looked at me and were like, “Do you have plans in August? We’d love to have you.“ I immediately called my mom and said, “...I think I got the job??!” Later that night, the official offer came through. That was such a cool feeling. Just being so connected to the character in the room, and on top of that, being saved from that horrible waiting game (every actor knows this one) of not knowing if I booked the job or not.

6. You said something along the lines of working with Ryan (Coogler) was like a dream come true. Are there any other directors or actors that you dream of working with one day? -anonymous

I have a running list…

7. Are you a sweets or savory girl? -Allison

This pregnancy has thrown this answer a little bit, but I’m a sweets girl through and through. It’s almost impossible for me not to have the tiniest something sweet after a meal.

8. Snacks to eat during an awards show? (To get ready for the Oscars🫣) -anonymous

I’m inclined to find something gold! My mouth is watering at these. Also, black tie brie bites? I loveee a crème brûlée. Have you ever made one? It’s fairly easy! The water bath part is a teeny bit tedious, but ingredient-wise it’s pretty simple. This is a good recipe. Add pomegranate seeds if you see fit.

9. Who are three co-stars you would want to work with again? -Abbie

Woody Harrelson, Anna Baryshnikov, and Viola Davis.

10. I know you love fashion! Could you list some of your favorite clothing brands? -Carolles

Some I love are: The Frankie Shop, COS, Éterne, Enza Costa, and of course, vintage.

11. If 15-year-old Hailee could see your life now, what do you think would shock her the most? -Angelica

I opt out of shocking my 15-year-old self. To echo Alysa Liu’s sentiment, she’s 15 and figuring it out, and she’ll get where she’s meant to be in the way she’s meant to get there.

TO SHOP: Our women-owned brands edit! This was fun to pull together. There’s a mix of smaller new-to-me brands — like Diarrablu, Blueland, and Wildgrain — and ones you already know and love, like Prada and Staud.

TO WRITE: More letters by hand. They’re simple, but I love these raw-edge notecards.

TO SLEEP: 6 daytime habits for better sleep. If Daylight Saving Time, while being glorious, has thrown off your zzzs.

TO POP: The best microwave popcorn, ranked by The Washington Post. Oscar’s Sunday, solved.

TO TAKE WITH YOU: “Stay close to any sounds that make you glad you are alive.” -Hafiz

Love,
Hailee
(Beau)

Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, Dave’s response to I’m in awe.

“We are all Sinners.” -Dave (MBS)

Subscribe for all the BS

If there's ever an opportunity to try an omakase experience, do it! This is big talk coming from a non-ambitious sushi eater, but now I want to get everyone in my life into sushi. My brother is a hard sell, and now I'm like, how can I crack the code to get him to try it? There’s this restaurant I love that has wagyu nigiri, so it's not technically sushi. I think if I can get him to eat that, then he'll be in! 3. Would you ever consider playing a villain or an antagonist in a movie? -Hannah Yes, of course! Know anyone hiring? 4. You’ve spoken so warmly about the cast of Sinners, what’s a moment from filming or press promo with them that still makes you smile?? -Soff I want to use a specific moment to answer this question. But at this moment, being two days away from the Oscars, I know there is no one single particular moment that can encapsulate what this filming experience meant to me. These people have left a mark on me and on my heart that will never go away. 5. What were your auditions like for the movie The Edge of Seventeen? -Ryan I don’t remember off the top of my head how many auditions I had. Let’s call it a handful, if that; it wasn’t anything crazy. The craziest part was that I was offered the job in the audition room. The last audition was just me, Kelly Fremon Craig, and James L. Brooks. I went through the reading, and I had this feeling that happens in a great audition, where I think, if nothing else happens from here, I’ll be fine. I’d be devastated for a minute, but I felt really good about what I did. Then Jim and Kelly looked at me and were like, “Do you have plans in August? We’d love to have you.“ I immediately called my mom and said, “...I think I got the job??!” Later that night, the official offer came through. That was such a cool feeling. Just being so connected to the character in the room, and on top of that, being saved from that horrible waiting game (every actor knows this one) of not knowing if I booked the job or not. 6. You said something along the lines of working with Ryan (Coogler) was like a dream come true. Are there any other directors or actors that you dream of working with one day? -anonymous I have a running list… 7. Are you a sweets or savory girl? -Allison This pregnancy has thrown this answer a little bit, but I’m a sweets girl through and through. It’s almost impossible for me not to have the tiniest something sweet after a meal. 8. Snacks to eat during an awards show? (To get ready for the Oscars🫣) -anonymous I’m inclined to find something gold! My mouth is watering at these. Also, black tie brie bites? I loveee a crème brûlée. Have you ever made one? It’s fairly easy! The water bath part is a teeny bit tedious, but ingredient-wise it’s pretty simple. This is a good recipe. Add pomegranate seeds if you see fit. 9. Who are three co-stars you would want to work with again? -Abbie Woody Harrelson, Anna Baryshnikov, and Viola Davis. 10. I know you love fashion! Could you list some of your favorite clothing brands? -Carolles Some I love are: The Frankie Shop, COS, Éterne, Enza Costa, and of course, vintage. 11. If 15-year-old Hailee could see your life now, what do you think would shock her the most? -Angelica I opt out of shocking my 15-year-old self. To echo Alysa Liu’s sentiment, she’s 15 and figuring it out, and she’ll get where she’s meant to be in the way she’s meant to get there. TO SHOP: Our women-owned brands edit! This was fun to pull together. There’s a mix of smaller new-to-me brands — like Diarrablu, Blueland, and Wildgrain — and ones you already know and love, like Prada and Staud. TO WRITE: More letters by hand. They’re simple, but I love these raw-edge notecards. TO SLEEP: 6 daytime habits for better sleep. If Daylight Saving Time, while being glorious, has thrown off your zzzs. TO POP: The best microwave popcorn, ranked by The Washington Post. Oscar’s Sunday, solved. TO TAKE WITH YOU: “Stay close to any sounds that make you glad you are alive.” -Hafiz Love,Hailee(Beau) Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, Dave’s response to I’m in awe. “We are all Sinners.” -Dave (MBS) Subscribe for all the BS

title: I’m in awe — BEAU SOCIETY issue #075
author: beausociety
raw_article:
Happy Friday!

Before I sat down on my couch to write to you, I pulled on one of my favorite big giant t-shirts: A Fear of God x Sinners collab, part of a small capsule merch collection that designer Jerry Lorenzo released for our film premiere. It feels only fitting to be wearing it as I sit here thinking about my friends and castmates who, over the past few weeks, have been receiving the highest honors in film, delivering incredible performances (I’ve had Miles’ Actor Awards performance and Tiny Desk on repeat), and turning out the most insane looks.

While I haven’t been able to attend the past few award shows, I’ve felt the radiant energy from my cast — they’re killing it! — and to say I’m in awe would be an understatement. Here are some of my favorite moments…

Every time I see Wunmi, she’s in the most breathtaking dress, gown, or ensemble. On top of being the warm light she is, Wunmi and her team have been intentional about highlighting Black female designers and artists throughout this press tour. Through her fashion choices, Wunmi has introduced me to some of the most beautiful Nigerian fashion brands, like RI MI, which means “see me” in Yorùbá, founded by Bukola Shoaga, a Nigerian-American, and Ahluwalia by Priya Ahluwalia, who’s a Nigerian-British designer.

Not only that, but Wunmi is currently pregnant with her second child and has been quietly working on designs for her own maternity clothing line called Iyadé. She knows her fashion. Have I told you this woman sewed her own wedding dress? She’s incredible.

And now, can we talk about Jayme Lawson?? It’s been so fun to see her shine through this award show run, too. She looks perfect and so herself in every photo.

Zinzi Coogler, producer on Sinners, nails the fashion game as well. (I still wish I’d gotten a photo of the shoes she wore to the Golden Globes.) And let’s not forget about Lola and LiLi.

In case you missed it, Wunmi, Jayme, and Zinzi graced the cover of Essence this month alongside Ruth E. Carter (costume designer for Sinners), Autumn Durald Arkapaw (the cinematographer on Sinners), Shunika Terry-Jennings (the head of hair on Sinners), and Hannah Beachler (the production designer on Sinners). I need this shot framed immediately.

Autumn was also on the cover of Vogue Philippines this week, looking elegant as ever. Read her full interview here.

And let’s take a moment for the guys…

On the awards front, Ryan took home a BAFTA for Best Original Screenplay. Miles won the award for Outstanding Breakthrough Performance in a Motion Picture at the NAACP Image Awards. Michael won the Actor Award for Outstanding Performance by a Male Actor, and Sinners won for Best Ensemble.

There’s so much more I could say about this inspiring cast and crew, but this week I’ve been smiling ear to ear more than usual. As we inch closer to the Oscars, I’m getting extra emotional and reflective about my time on this set with these people. I’m going to go through my archives to see what else I have…

TO WATCH: Unseen footage of our cast’s hair and makeup test.

TO LISTEN TO: Miles Caton and blues legend Buddy Guy on Tiny Desk. On repeat.

TO WEAR: Sinners Fear of God merch. It was a limited edition capsule, but you can find some resale gems on eBay.

TO TAKE WITH YOU: “Always clap for your friends even if their dreams transpire before yours.” -Dhar Mann

Love,
Hailee
(Beau)

Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, Soff’s response to The Sound of February.

“The way you constantly write about enjoying small moments feels like a reminder to me that it’s okay to slow down and just go with the wind until you find a direction. Not everything you do in life has to be meticulously planned, and sometimes what you need is just to stop for a while, take a look at what you have achieved, breathe deep, and trust that all will be okay.

Loved this read and thank you for another beau-tiful issue Beau! 💗 (Will be trying out your shrimp Caesar salad, it honestly looks delicious)” -Soff (More BS member)

Subscribe for all the BS

title: The Sound of February — BEAU SOCIETY issue #074
author: beausociety
raw_article:
This week, I was scheduling a meeting for next week and checked my calendar to find that next week is March! I don’t know how that happened. January was three years long, and February passed in a few days. At least that’s what it felt like to me. Whatever wizardry has been happening, this is the last BS issue of February, so I thought it might be fun to do a little recap of my favorite things from this month.

This list is incredibly random from my no. 1 pregnancy craving (not excited about it) to the show I binged this week against my better judgment. I hope you find a good rec or two!

My no.1 pregnancy craving...

It’s shrimp. Like I said, not thrilled by it. But alas, a Caesar salad with grilled shrimp has been a huge hit for me lately. The other night, we met some friends for an early dinner, and the place we went to does a great Caesar with Parmesan crisps and croutons. Normally, I add chicken, but of course, given my craving, it had to be shrimp.

An easy 6-episode binge with Sophie Turner

Have you seen Steal? It’s a mini-series on Amazon Prime. It’s just six episodes. We’d heard great things, so we added it to our watchlist…easiest binge ever. I love Sophie Turner so much. She’s such a talent. The show is awesome, although by watching it, I’m going against my own advice of ‘don’t watch anything overstimulating before bed.’ I just couldn’t resist.

The Sound of Music rewatch

I highly recommend starting a list of old movies you love that you haven’t rewatched in years. Maybe you can’t quite recall the exact story, but you know at one point or another, you loved it. When you need something to watch, instead of going straight to new releases, pick one from your personal archive. I have that list, and the other night, I rewatched The Sound of Music, and wow, that movie is magic. I can’t tell you the last time I saw it, but it all comes back to you.

Just lying in the grass

I haven’t felt up for a whooole lot recently, but some days just walking in the backyard and lying down in the grass is everything I need: fresh air and sunshine.

Jeans that are kind of a joke

The “jeans” I’m wearing above are those Rag & Bone ones. I forgot to mention them in my recent outfit roundup, but I went from thinking they were embarrassing to wearing them all the time.

Ponytail braids

My hair is at such a great length right now — I’m so happy I can finally do a braid! Lately, I’ve been doing a thick ponytail and then braiding it. When I take my hair down, it still looks decent. I don’t have a picture, but it’s like this.

Tea & honey

My recent favorite drink: tea with honey...like a ton of honey. When I like a new tea, I try to take a picture of the packet label so I can rebuy it. I like the Harney & Sons Organic Chai and this ginger peach white tea. I try to opt for tea without caffeine.

Short nails

I recently chopped my nails shorter than usual. They were so grown out, and I was just over them. I don’t have a picture to share because they’re offensively uneven, but I will try to get one once I clean them up. I think I’m going to keep them short for a while.

Love-day breakfast

This was our lovely, perfect little Valentine’s Day breakfast. I’m very proud of how the French Toast came out.

TO MAKE: This buffalo chicken dip (in a crockpot!) that came to us from a reader recommendation - thanks, Caitlyn!

TO WEAR: Something that feels like spring. May I suggest this dress?

TO TAKE WITH YOU: Just a reminder, rushing never helps.

Love,
Hailee
(Beau)

Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, Emmaline’s response to Reality settles in.

I loved this issue and getting a glimpse into how you’re preparing for the baby! Also, the quote from Rumi at the end is so true. We rarely take the time to be quiet and listen as much as we should! -Emmaline (More BS reader)

Subscribe for all the BS

title: Reality settles in — BEAU SOCIETY issue #073
author: beausociety
raw_article:
Hi, my loves!

Happy Friday, wherever you are.

I’m excited to say we are dropping a second newsletter early next week for our special MORE BS community. That letter will be coming separately to your inbox. Let’s just say, it’s about time to size up in my jeans…For those who have subscribed, thank you! If you haven’t, and you want more, you can join us here.

Subscribe now

This week, I was sitting on the couch in my family room, and the reality that our little family is about to grow really started to settle in. Of course, I have all the to-do lists (which you know I love), and I’ve been busy with research and the general life reorganization that happens when you’re about to have a baby. But the most profound and grounding part of getting ready for this baby has been the introspective side of it: the quiet conversations I’m having with myself almost every day. Sometimes I find myself asking big, lofty questions (what am I going to be like as a mom?) or having vivid daydreams of what our life is about to look like when the three of us are cuddled up in the nursery together. While tuning in, I’ve experienced this surreal, full-body awareness that our world is about to expand in beautiful ways. We’re getting ready to meet someone we already love so much. I’ve racked my brain for words to describe that feeling, and I don’t have them yet. Maybe that’s the point. For now, I’m blessed to be settling into the feeling.

Nesting

On a more practical note, the nesting phase is real, and I’m in the middle of it. Unsurprisingly, putting together a nursery has been a joy for me. I’ve gone back and forth on tiny details more than I care to admit (why is choosing a rocking chair this emotional?), but seeing it all slowly come together has made our new life feel more tangible. Emphasis on ‘slowly’ coming together — it’s all still very much a work in progress.

Between helpful texts, voice notes, and the passing “you’ll thank me later” advice, I’m starting to feel more prepared for a newborn. Plus, last weekend my mom threw the most beautiful baby shower at our home (more to come on that next week in MORE BS), which added to the “this is really happening” feeling.

While I know that, like pregnancy, every newborn journey is unique — and what one person swears by might not work for you — there are a few essentials almost every mom friend has urged me to have ready:

  • Double zipper sleepers (I’ve heard them described as “life-changing”)

  • More crib sheets than you think you’ll need

  • More burp clothes than you think you’ll need

  • A sound machine

  • A really good rocking chair (I’m getting teary again!)

  • A postpartum basket for me (this one is always said emphatically)

Generally, I have a less-is-more approach to shopping for baby clothes. I don’t want to be overwhelmed by stuff. However, I’ve done a little bit. There’s something surreal about folding tiny clothes, organizing drawers, placing little socks in a basket, and thinking, our baby will actually wear this(!!!). These are some of my favorite baby brands I’ve discovered so far:

  • Caden Lane

  • Kyte Baby

  • This Little Piggy Wears Vintage (we love vintage around here)

  • Burt’s Bees (I’ve been told these burp cloths are a must)

  • Carter’s

The list goes on. To all the moms, what are your favorites??

An endnote on love

Earlier this week, over in the More BS chat, we had a lively discussion about love. We posed the question, “When do you feel most loved?” and the chat went off. I wanted to share some of the answers because they’re a great reminder that there are a million and one ways to feel and show love.

When do you feel most loved?

When someone remembers a detail most don’t -Kara

Most definitely, when someone just listens…just a simple thing like that -Alice

​​When my nieces randomly come up to me and give me a hug 🥰 -Brandy

When someone shares my interests and hobbies, and then brings something new to the table💞 -Konny

When my friends let me be myself. Or, when they check up on me when I’m not myself, so I know I always have someone there for me. -Char

Just by being seen and heard, honestly 😅 I’m a little wary of love, but I’m trying to be more open -Sammi

When I choose to show up for myself, even when I’m feeling low, and I remember my worth and how far I’ve come✨ -md_blondie

I feel the most loved when I am seen beyond my profession. -Katy

When I am surrounded by people I love. -Lesley

TO EAT: The French toast we whipped up for Valentine’s Day breakfast. 10/10 will make again.

TO DRINK: This alcohol-free champagne has been my go-to “drink” through pregnancy during those frustrating moments when I crave my favorite margarita.

TO TRY: Writing a “ta-da” list. Because your to-do list is long enough (talking to myself).

TO WEAR: A great pair of lug-sole rainboots. While holed up this week, I started shopping for actually-cute rain gear and found some gems (literally).

TO TAKE WITH YOU: “The quieter you become, the more you are able to hear.” -Rumi

Love,
Hailee
(Beau)

MORE BS: Don’t forget we’re dropping our first exclusive MORE BS letter at the top of next week. Don’t miss it.

Sign up for MORE BS

Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, Karina’s response to Things I love, especially on Valentine’s Day.

Yes, an issue on love languages would be amazing! My husband and I read The Five Love Languages by Gary Chapman every couple of years. It’s such a great refresher for us. Especially since we are in such a busy season of life with 2 kids and another one on the way. -Karina (More BS member)

Cover photography by Kat Temkin.

(Beau) MORE BS: Don’t forget we’re dropping our first exclusive MORE BS letter at the top of next week. Don’t miss it. Sign up for MORE BS Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, Karina’s response to Things I love, especially on Valentine’s Day. Yes, an issue on love languages would be amazing! My husband and I read The Five Love Languages by Gary Chapman every couple of years. It’s such a great refresher for us. Especially since we are in such a busy season of life with 2 kids and another one on the way. -Karina (More BS member) Cover photography by Kat Temkin.

title: Things I love, especially on Valentine's Day — BEAU SOCIETY issue #072
author: beausociety
raw_article:
I love February. I don’t know if it’s the pink and red everywhere (forever a fave color combo), the chocolate cake and cookie recipes that pop up the second I open my phone, or the way every streaming service suddenly becomes a curated shrine to romance. Maybe it’s the simple fact that I love love and any excuse to celebrate it: romantic, platonic, self-love, dog love…it all plays. A cherry on top is having a husband who not only tolerates my enthusiasm for this holiday but genuinely leans into it with me. Lucky for me, he’s also perfectly satisfied with the version of the day that results in both of us in sweats on the couch doing a whole lot of nothing.

Ahead, I’ve assembled an extensive list of Valentine’s Day thoughts and ideas. Nothing is extravagant or overly ambitious. But let’s celebrate that we’ve arrived at the new year, giving us a fresh start. What better way to lead than with love and the energy that comes with giving and receiving it!

TO DO:

Here’s a shortlist of potential Valentine’s Day activities. Some came my way from friends (both partnered and single), others from my own life. Consider it an extremely flexible itinerary.

  1. Take in the sunrise. This is completely free and starts your day with an overwhelming sense of awe (a close sister to love). Wake up early, make your favorite coffee or tea, step outside, and watch the sky changing color. It’s a deceptively magical, early win.

  2. Enjoy a long, luxurious bath or shower. Capitalize on relaxation today. If you’re a bath person like I am, draw one and add Epsom salts. Put on some chill music (I might opt for oldies love songs — my wedding-day getting-ready playlist!). Float for a while. When you get out, slather your body with this oil. It’s so good. If you’re looking for a more extensive bath guide, check this out.

  3. Savor quiet time. Curl up with a book. If you have a partner, ask if they want to join. I find those moments of being together in silence deeply romantic.

  4. Check in with someone you love who’s far away. This might be a long-distance best friend or a grandparent you don’t get to see often. FaceTime or call and tell them you love them.

  5. Play in the kitchen. Find a new recipe, split up the tasks, assign head chef and sous chef, and go to town. (If you’re solo cooking, you can be the head and sous chef - powerful.)

  6. Be offline. Brick your phone or delete social media for the day. Comparing yourself to others is not romantic.

  7. Make something sweet. Chocolate chip cookies with pink M&Ms or raspberries with a chocolate chip inside. Doesn’t have to be fancy.

  8. Light a candle. There’s kind of no better occasion for dinner by candlelight. Scroll ahead for my favorite tapered candles.

  9. Wear pink or red. Find the way that feels like you. Maybe that’s painting your nails a deep red or wearing a pink scarf for your morning coffee walk.

  1. Write a love note. “I made you a coffee - it’s in the fridge,” “You’re a rockstar,” or simply “I love you! :)”

  2. Remember, this is just another day. If this holiday feels pressured, acknowledge that, and try to let it go. If you end up watching the Olympics on the couch in your pajamas all day, and that brings you joy, you’re doing it right!

TO WATCH:

These are a few of my favorite films that you can’t go wrong watching any time of year, but feel especially perfect for an at-home matinee this weekend.

  • The classic rom-com: When Harry Met Sally

  • ‘80s nostalgia: Pretty in Pink

  • Adam Sandler: 50 First Dates

  • For book lovers: Notting Hill

  • ‘90s nostalgia: Love & Basketball

  • Old Hollywood: Casablanca

  • Always reliable: Crazy, Stupid Love

  • Meg Ryan (in Paris!): French Kiss

  • Hot summer: Dirty Dancing

  • Pure beauty: In the Mood for Love

TO EAT:

We’re still working on our Valentine’s Day dinner/menu situation. I’m feeling inspired by the below options…plus some Angel Margarita, requested by my husband. He suggests our readers enjoy the Lime with some Tajín on the rim. Pro tip.

  • Angel margarita

  • One-pot spaghetti al limone

  • Homemade pizza

  • Garlic butter steak

  • Roasted tomato and burrata caprese

  • Charlie Bird’s farro salad

  • Skillet brownies

  • Olive oil chocolate chip cookies

  • Homemade whipped cream

Ambitious idea, but I love it: Recreate the exact meal you had on your first date! If you do this, please write to me and tell me how it goes.

TO GIFT:

A little something small and indulgent for someone you love…could be yourself!

  • A brushed cashmere beanie to carry you through the rest of winter

  • The prettiest tapered candles you’ve ever seen

  • A gorgeous (and bra-free!) midi dress in a poppy red

  • A subscription to a local flower shop

  • A vintage, fully restored rotary desk phone

  • Metrograph membership for two

  • A baby shower gift(!) I’m obsessed with: this little heart pillow

  • Cool date-night top

  • A great pair of red-frame sunnies

  • Pink velvet ballet flats, I love

  • Boyfriend socks

  • A packable quilted pullover in bright pink

  • A heart locket

  • My go-to tinted lip balm

  • New pajamas

  • Pencils for love notes

TO KNOW ABOUT: The sleep habits of Olympians. Are you loving the Winter Games or what?

TO COMBO: Crimson and cream.

TAKE WITH YOU: “Front-load your weird. The right person will stay.” -Mark Manson

Let me know what you have planned for Valentine’s Day in the comments. Should we do an issue on love languages?? Seems like it might be fun to dig into!

LOVE YOU ALL!

XOXO,

Hailee
(Beau)

Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, Glacias’s response to You asked, I answered.

“This community is everything to me 💗. I wake up every Saturday (cuz I’m from Aus), excited to read your article, and I especially love this week’s quote. I feel like obsessions are the only thing that keeps the world alive🥰! Can’t wait for next week’s issue!” -Glacia (More BS member)

title: You asked, I answered — BEAU SOCIETY issue #071
author: beausociety
raw_article:
Hello!

Happy February! January felt six months long, didn’t it? But closing out the first week of February, I’m finally feeling the Year of the Horse energy.

If you’re looking for signs of positive momentum, look no further than More BS group chat, where you’ll find an active community of BS readers from all over the world swapping film, music, cooking, and travel recommendations. Everyone’s getting involved. Midweek, I dropped an AMA in our members’ chat (which I plan to do regularly), and the questions that came in were so thoughtful and specific, I wanted to elaborate on my answers in this week’s issue. This More BS group is already proving to be a smart, savvy, curious bunch. My kind of people.

What’s one piece of advice that you’ve carried with you through your career?

-Deborah

When I was 13 on the set of my first movie, True Grit, Jeff Bridges, Matt Damon, and Josh Brolin all gave me the same piece of career advice, in slightly different ways. They said the secret, the key to the game, is simple: HAVE FUN. They led by example. These guys came to work every day, had so much fun making a movie and doing what they love, and when we wrapped, they went home to their families. They told me not to get caught up in the minutia (learned that word on set!) of the business of performing and instead focus on doing what I love and having as much fun as I can while doing it.

Of course, when you’re 30, hearing “just have fun and your career will magically appear” doesn’t really hit. You’ve lived enough life to know that work is often hard, exhausting, and doesn’t follow a clear path. But at this stage of my life, I believe if you love what you do for a career and it lights you up, it can never be the wrong thing.

How do you maintain an intentional relationship while both of you are so busy?

-Bridget

I love this question. There’s an easy answer in my brain. I don’t want it to seem cliché, but we focus on being truly present with each other. More specifically, we’re big on weekly date nights, and going no phones for an hour at the end of the day.

What’s a small habit that low-key changed your life?

-Md_blondie

You know the saying, ‘if you love someone, tell them’? I’ve tried to adapt that mindset into a habit. For example, if I notice something admirable about a person — their hard work or a certain skillset I don’t have — and I know sharing the compliment will affect them positively, I tell them. I’ve learned that so much good comes back to you when you don’t hold those things in.

What part of the creative process do you wish fans understood more?

-Angelica

I wish more people understood the value of experiencing a big reveal. Today, most movies are boxed into a 60-second trailer that gives everything away. We forget that hundreds, if not thousands, of creative people have worked tirelessly for months, if not years, to make a movie designed to sweep us off our feet and move us in a way that requires our full attention for more than 60 seconds. I appreciate it when fans go to a movie without watching the trailer. Go and be along for the ride. That’s what it’s there for.

I love your issues about hosting and can tell you’re very good at it. Best hosting advice to ensure a smooth and fun time for everyone?

-Isabelle

First of all, thank you! This is a fun one. I’m of two minds here. I would say make sure to give yourself enough time to plan. But on the other hand, I love it when something great comes together at the last minute. For the latter (which I prefer), all you really need is a great group of people. Start there. Then add great food, great music, and a great atmosphere, which I feel like is created by the people — you could be anywhere. Regardless of how elaborate the gathering is, I’m almost always starting with a mood board or a physical to-do list.

Do you ever see yourself coming back to music?

-Deborah

While I have no plans to make music right at this exact moment, do I think about it? All the time. Music has always been a huge part of my life, and if it makes sense to get back in a studio, it’s an easy yes. But the music industry has changed quite a bit over the last few years. I don’t feel as creatively fulfilled by music as much as I used to. I love the way Charli XCX writes about this.

If you had to choose any film that you were in that was your all-time favorite. What would it be?

-Anthony

I have three answers. True Grit, because it was my first film. The Edge of Seventeen, because it felt like a turning point in my life. As an actor, that role brought me closer to myself. Then, Sinners. That was a role that I’d been wishing would come into my life at some point, and it came at the perfect time.

What part of acting still scares you, even now?

-Olga

All of it. Showing up on the first day of a set and feeling like I’ve never acted before in my life. Acting also has an innate way of constantly pushing you towards, if not over, your limits…mentally, emotionally, physically. That can be scary. But oftentimes it’s the best part.

What do your workouts look like now that you’re pregnant?

-Meredith

At this point, they’re a little slower. I’m prioritizing listening to my body, which has been a consistent thing I’ve always done when it comes to working out, regardless of my physical state. Recently, I’ve been doing a lot of walking. Sometimes I set out for 30 minutes, and it ends up being 15, or less than that. I’m taking it slow, but movement is key. The other day, I felt like I should be going up and down the stairs more, so I started doing some housework that had me going up and down the stairs, and afterwards I was like, ‘Okay, I’m good with stairs for a while!’

What’s something you had to unlearn to grow as an artist?

-Angelica

Not to be so careful. I can be such a cautious, careful person. It’s not about being ‘careless’ by any means as an artist, but I try not to be as much of a rule-follower or a people-pleaser. Being intentional and conscious is a good thing, but not to the extent that it holds me back from living fearlessly.

What is your hair care routine like? I’m suffering so much with breakage and struggle to find good products to help my hair retain moisture.

-Nicole

I’ve been using and loving my Nexxus shampoo with hyaluronic acid, which is extremely moisturizing and helps when I’m traveling back and forth between cold and warm climates. I’m grateful that my hair has been growing extra fast lately — I’m sure the pregnancy and prenatals are helping there. As for other hair products I’m obsessed with, Uni also makes amazing shampoo and conditioner. I’ve also been using their 24-hour Body Serum during my pregnancy, and it’s amazing.

Thank you all for being here. I love these AMA issues because they help me connect with all of you (and with myself) more deeply. Enjoy your weekend!

Love,
Hailee
(Beau)

TO CHECK OUT: Your February Love Horoscope.

TO READ: The Art of Being Unreachable.

TO GET INSPIRED BY: The cold-weather street style at Copenhagen Fashion Week.

TO WATCH: This Sunday’s Super Bowl commercials!

TO ENJOY: A cool, limey margarita! Angel’s now available at Ralph’s in SoCal!!

TO TAKE WITH YOU: “Obsessions are the only things that matter.” -Patricia Highsmith

Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, we hear E. Logan’s response to Introducing More BS.

“I love the quote at the end. I’ve been trying to be better about being more present. I wish I actually had a plug to pull sometimes 😅 Excited for what’s to come with BS!! ♥️” -E. Logan

Subscribe for all the BS

title: Introducing MORE BS — BEAU SOCIETY issue #070
author: beausociety
raw_article:
Hi, my loves!

I’ve been publishing this weekly newsletter for a year and a half. In that time, I’ve learned so much about myself — about my style, taste, and what lights me up creatively at this stage of my life. Through the weekly repetition of jotting down ideas for this letter, then writing, editing, and designing it (with the help of my tiny but incredible team), Beau Society has grown into something that feels more like me than anything I’ve done before. But really, that’s a testament to all of you.

Never in a million years did I expect that so many of you would read this letter and take the time to send a thoughtful reply about how it resonated with you. But you have! Since the very beginning, Beau Society has felt like a two-way conversation, a growing community coming together, and we’re ready to take it a layer deeper.

This week, after months of planning, Beau Society is moving into a new chapter. This next step will help me (and my small but mighty team) give you more of everything: more letters, more conversations, and more opportunities to connect with other readers from around the world.

Today, we’re activating a membership tier called MORE BS. If you sign up, you’ll continue to receive the full Beau Society issue every Friday, plus you’ll get the behind-the-scenes extras, like photos that didn’t make the letter and on-the-fly voicemails from wherever I am in the world. Maybe most excitingly, you’ll also get access to a group chat exclusively for MORE BS members, where we’ll be able to talk daily instead of weekly, inside this smaller, more intimate community.

Join MORE BS

If you don’t want to join MORE BS, all good — the Beau Society newsletter will continue to land in your inbox each Friday as usual! But if you decide you want the good stuff inside the membership tier, you know where to find us (upgrade your subscription in the Substack app).

Thank you so much for being here and reading and commenting and loving Beau Society as much as I do.

Here’s to MORE BS!

TO WEAR: The best pajamas. These were a Christmas gift from my husband, and I’m wondering how I didn’t come across this brand pre-pregnancy.

TO SAVE YOUR LIPS: Greer put me onto this incredible lip serum that has kept my lip skin from flaking off this winter. Lip serum > balm.

TO CRAFT: Your own needlepoint! In last week’s comment section, a reader sent a recommendation for Bargello Needlepoint (thanks, Caroline!). It looks like they have a full range of beginner canvases. This one is calling to me.

TO TAKE WITH YOU: “Almost everything will start working again if you unplug it for a few minutes… including you.” -Anne Lamott

COMMUNITY MOMENT

I’ve always been fascinated by other people’s in-flight essentials and travel hacks. A few weeks ago, I asked for the one thing you need when traveling. Some of your must-haves include:

Global entry -Amanda

A pashmina -Ann

A journal — ask strangers to write life advice in it! -Logan

Colgate wisps -Isabelle

A pillowcase -Hannah

Having a capsule wardrobe -Jessica

The roll-clothes method -Yols

Packing cubes -Alex

A convert-money calculator -Sandra

Some chocolates for the flight crew! -Ann (this may be my favorite!)

Love,
Hailee
(Beau)

P.S. We’re back to our regularly scheduled programming on Friday. Talk then — or sooner, in the chat!

Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, we hear Char’s response to The most nominated movie in Oscars history.

“Sweet 16 FOR REAL! This is such an amazing achievement, and ever since the very beginning, the love and soul were felt through every aspect of the film. This movie has truly changed lives, and for me, it’s a reminder to keep doing what I’m doing to make my own way in this industry, as only great things are waiting.” -Char

p_text: Hi, my loves! I’ve been publishing this weekly newsletter for a year and a half. In that time, I’ve learned so much about myself — about my style, taste, and what lights me up creatively at this stage of my life. Through the weekly repetition of jotting down ideas for this letter, then writing, editing, and designing it (with the help of my tiny but incredible team), Beau Society has grown into something that feels more like me than anything I’ve done before. But really, that’s a testament to all of you. Never in a million years did I expect that so many of you would read this letter and take the time to send a thoughtful reply about how it resonated with you. But you have! Since the very beginning, Beau Society has felt like a two-way conversation, a growing community coming together, and we’re ready to take it a layer deeper. This week, after months of planning, Beau Society is moving into a new chapter. This next step will help me (and my small but mighty team) give you more of everything: more letters, more conversations, and more opportunities to connect with other readers from around the world. Today, we’re activating a membership tier called MORE BS. If you sign up, you’ll continue to receive the full Beau Society issue every Friday, plus you’ll get the behind-the-scenes extras, like photos that didn’t make the letter and on-the-fly voicemails from wherever I am in the world. Maybe most excitingly, you’ll also get access to a group chat exclusively for MORE BS members, where we’ll be able to talk daily instead of weekly, inside this smaller, more intimate community. Join MORE BS If you don’t want to join MORE BS, all good — the Beau Society newsletter will continue to land in your inbox each Friday as usual! But if you decide you want the good stuff inside the membership tier, you know where to find us (upgrade your subscription in the Substack app). Thank you so much for being here and reading and commenting and loving Beau Society as much as I do. Here’s to MORE BS! TO WEAR: The best pajamas. These were a Christmas gift from my husband, and I’m wondering how I didn’t come across this brand pre-pregnancy. TO SAVE YOUR LIPS: Greer put me onto this incredible lip serum that has kept my lip skin from flaking off this winter. Lip serum > balm. TO CRAFT: Your own needlepoint! In last week’s comment section, a reader sent a recommendation for Bargello Needlepoint (thanks, Caroline!). It looks like they have a full range of beginner canvases. This one is calling to me. TO TAKE WITH YOU: “Almost everything will start working again if you unplug it for a few minutes… including you.” -Anne Lamott I’ve always been fascinated by other people’s in-flight essentials and travel hacks. A few weeks ago, I asked for the one thing you need when traveling. Some of your must-haves include: Global entry -Amanda A pashmina -Ann A journal — ask strangers to write life advice in it! -Logan Colgate wisps -Isabelle A pillowcase -Hannah Having a capsule wardrobe -Jessica The roll-clothes method -Yols Packing cubes -Alex A convert-money calculator -Sandra Some chocolates for the flight crew! -Ann (this may be my favorite!) Love,Hailee(Beau) P.S. We’re back to our regularly scheduled programming on Friday. Talk then — or sooner, in the chat! Highlighting your comments on the previous week’s issue. Today, we hear Char’s response to The most nominated movie in Oscars history. “Sweet 16 FOR REAL! This is such an amazing achievement, and ever since the very beginning, the love and soul were felt through every aspect of the film. This movie has truly changed lives, and for me, it’s a reminder to keep doing what I’m doing to make my own way in this industry, as only great things are waiting.” -Char Subscribe for all the BS
